item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad I like it also this is my first reply to yippi

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a lot of detail going into this without much in the way of an actual plot. It spends a lot of wordspace going over the specific criterion by which it collects and liquefies gold, without doing anything interesting with it. It's very needing of a hook or twist of some kind.

item.title: License Enforcment
item.content:encoded:
Image Source
Author: Bill Bouton
CC-BY-SA 2.0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This desperately needs a hook of some kind, or some detail that catches the eye, because 'magpie that purifies gold' is just not all that interesting on its own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One question. Does the bird actively go out and mine for gold? If not, how does the bird accumulate gold in -1?

Fix this, and I will upvote. As other mentioned, and I agree, this is a neat article.

item.title: One small detail
item.content:encoded:
2389 shows a desire for collecting objects of at least 8-carat gold and will collect all objects meeting this requirement it comes across.

You are missing the SCP- at the start.

Which is also the only detail out of place. The rest is sweet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In retrospect, I find the more mundane theory of mating to have its charm. We are too used to the Wow and plot twists to escalate things. I think it is a nice touch to go for a more mundane means.

That said, I wish the mating part is more developed… or if the mate is revealed to be something unexpected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reads a little lackluster in my opinion. It doesn't really standout to me. -1 for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After all the feedback I have received and, with second thought, I have removed the second addendum. The rest of the article remains untouched.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A well made safe-class article that works without having to rely on being strange or creepy.

I am upvoting, but like a lot of other users have said, it'd work better without the second addendum.

item.title: Agreed here
item.content:encoded: #2 didn't seem very foundation-y so I decided not to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The last addendum is rather questionable to say the least. I do not think they will use this SCP to obtain pure gold for funds; the money to be obtained via this method would be pocket change compared to the Foundation utilising other SCPs for more direct results. Furthermore, it is only one specimen and that is not enough to make enough profits for a group on the Foundation's scale.

I do appreciate you implying it might be for mating, but I do not appreciate you letting that hang and unaddressed. It would be a better end-point than what we have currently.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some testing logs involving the offspring would be cool. It's a good article even without that, though. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So this article left me a little torn. I can get behind the idea of a bird that collects gold and then anomalously weeds out the impurities, but the addenda just kind of go into a really silly lolFoundation place. A global organization on the scale of the Foundation need not a gold purity bird to fund its operations.

That aside, I'll err on the side of an upvote. The main effect is neat.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. It's specific enough that it's unique but vague enough that it could fit well into any narrative or tale that someone would want to use it for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Then" is correct there. Its a sequencer/attribution thing- "x will then cause y to happen after z happens." You could say "X happens after Y" but it weakens the attribution and tense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It started off well, at first I thought it was cool and interesting. I was going to upvote since it was a good example of an interesting scip, but slowly it just got bland and the second addendum just completely took away any chances of me upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait was that my suggestion?
On a side note, are you planning to add testing logs with the offspring?
+1'd, btw. I love this bird a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this idea. Well executed however:

SCP-2389 will then remove the solid impurities from SCP-2389-1 once the process is complete

I believe the word "then" should be removed from that sentence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea of using this to finance the Foundation's operations was actually a suggestion on my first draft in the forums (believe me when I say the original addendum was way more lolFoundation), but personally I think it would make sense. The Foundation needs to build secure facilities, purchase the materials to do so, pay their employees ridiculous sums and probably a load of other stuff I've failed to mention, all of which require ridiculous sums of money.

If the Foundation can be sure an anomaly is, to a degree, safe to use and can be used to finance operations of the Foundation then I feel that this would be exploited. Just disposing of the pure gold would be putting it to waste - The Foundation could easily make money from selling it. Although the Foundation contains things that are very abnormal, the Foundation needs to think realistically - long term budgeting, logistics and so forth.

But thank you for liking the article, it's much appreciated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this overall. The addendum smacks a bit of lolFoundation - the question of how it finances its operations doesn't seem to fit the general tone of the site in my headcanon. Maybe it would work better as a question of how to dispose of the purified gold.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't mind this. It's an interesting idea, and better than some other SCPs which have made the list. It's well-written, and I can't see any real reason to downvote it.

+1
